candidates.item: Built a VIX-equivalent volatility index for Brazil's B3 market from scratch, adapting CBOE VIX methodology to messy/incomplete Brazilian options data. Used PostgreSQL, options chain API, daily calculations, compared against Ibovespa actual volatility. Key insight: pragmatic approximations beat strict textbook adherence when data is inconsistent.
candidates.why_interesting: Building a VIX clone is non-trivial even with clean US data — doing it for Brazil, where options data is sparse and inconsistent, and consciously departing from the CBOE textbook methodology to make it actually work, shows real engineering judgment. It's a specific, hard thing most quant hobbyists wouldn't finish.
candidates.recency_note: Site appears new/unindexed; project date unknown

candidates.item: Proof-of-concept smart contract version of the traditional Brazilian consórcio savings product. Deployed and verified on LACHAIN Testnet (contract visible on explorer). Key insight: surfaces gap between protocol design and production-ready deployment — highlights need for test coverage and threat modeling.
candidates.why_interesting: The consórcio is a deeply Brazilian financial institution (group savings/lottery product with no direct equivalent in most markets) — putting it on-chain is a clever cultural-meets-crypto idea that almost no one outside Brazil would even think to attempt. The honesty about the gap between proof-of-concept and production is also unusually candid for a portfolio.
candidates.recency_note: Deployed on LACHAIN Testnet; date unknown

candidates.item: Replicates methodology from academic paper 'Financial Statement Analysis with Large Language Models' adapted to Brazilian public companies. Uses OpenAI GPT-4 via API, SQL database of income statements/balance sheets, custom prompt templates, multi-year trend analysis. Key insight: prompt structure and data preparation materially affect LLM financial reasoning quality.
candidates.why_interesting: Rather than building a generic 'AI stock picker,' Pedro took a specific peer-reviewed paper and stress-tested its methodology against a non-US, emerging-market context (Brazil) where the paper was never designed to apply. That replication-and-stress-test framing is academically sharp and rare in portfolio projects.
candidates.recency_note: Site appears new/unindexed; project date unknown

candidates.item: Multi-agent AI system for Brazilian stock analysis, adapted from virattt's hedge-fund-agent-team, using BRAPI API. Each agent specializes in a different aspect of financial analysis. Key insight: agent specialization improves modularity but system is sensitive to prompt design; best used as analytical copilot.
candidates.why_interesting: He explicitly names the open-source repo he forked (virattt's hedge-fund-agent-team) rather than obscuring it — and then documents what broke and what the system is actually good for ('analytical copilot,' not autonomous fund). That level of intellectual honesty in a portfolio is distinctive and rare.
candidates.recency_note: Site appears new/unindexed; project date unknown

candidates.item: The project named 'DELOS' shares its name with the organization, suggesting this may be the flagship or namesake project — a blockchain-based oracle and tokenized securities system for Brazilian market infrastructure.
candidates.why_interesting: Naming your whole lab after a single project implies it's the one Pedro considers most serious or most ambitious. A tokenized-securities oracle aimed at Brazilian market infrastructure is a live regulatory and technical frontier (Brazil's CVM has been actively working on tokenization). The truncated description makes it the most intriguing unknown on the site.
candidates.recency_note: Description truncated on site; full scope unknown
